title: Asking for directions
subtitle: Getting around with the grammar you know

hook.text: Asking for and giving directions is one of the most practical skills in any language. You already have all the grammar you need: где (where), идти/ехать (go on foot/by vehicle), в/на + prepositional (location), imperatives (turn, go). This lesson is about applying what you know to a real situation.

why.text: Directions in Russian use куда (where to, with movement verbs) vs где (where, for location), and the motion verb pair идти (go on foot) / ехать (go by vehicle). The imperative forms of these are идите (go!) and поверните (turn!). The prepositions направо / налево / прямо are fixed adverbs — no case change needed.

cards.title: Asking where something is
cards.body: Use где + subject to ask location. For places, you already know prepositional case with в/на. Скажите is a polite opener before asking a stranger for directions.

examples.meaning: Excuse me, where is the train station?
Where is the metro located? (находится = is located)
How do I get to the centre? (до + genitive = to/up to)

cards.title: Direction adverbs and imperatives
cards.body: These adverbs never change form. Pair them with imperative verbs for giving directions. идите (go, on foot) and поверните (turn) are the two core imperative verbs for directions.

cards.title: Proximity and distance
cards.body: To say something is near or far, use рядом (nearby) and далеко (far) as predicates. To say near a landmark, use рядом с + instrumental.

examples.meaning: The metro is next to the station. (рядом с + instrumental)

questions.prompt: How do you ask 'where is the metro?'
questions.explanation: где + noun in nominative. Где метро?
идти → formal imperative = идите. прямо = straight. Идите прямо.
рядом (ryadom) = nearby / next to. Invariable adverb.

cards.title: идти vs ехать — on foot vs by vehicle
cards.body: идти = go on foot. ехать = go by vehicle (bus, car, metro). When giving walking directions: идите. When giving transport directions: езжайте (formal imperative of ехать). At A1 level, идти/идите covers most situations.

examples.meaning: Go (by vehicle) to the next stop.

cards.title: A full directions exchange
cards.body: Here is a complete example interaction — asking and receiving directions. Notice how the structures combine naturally.

examples.meaning: — Excuse me, where is the bank?

examples.cyrillic: — Идите прямо, потом поверните направо. Банк рядом.
examples.transliteration: — Idite pryamo, potom povernite napravo. Bank ryadom.
examples.meaning: — Go straight, then turn right. The bank is nearby.

examples.cyrillic: — Нет, это недалеко. Минут пять пешком.
examples.transliteration: — Net, eto nedaleko. Minut pyat' peshkom.
examples.meaning: — No, not far. About five minutes on foot.

questions.explanation: налево = to the left. Поверните налево.

questions.prompt: Excuse me, where is the bank? (formal, polite)
questions.explanation: Скажите (tell me, formal imperative) + пожалуйста + где + place.

questions.prompt: Go straight then turn right: Идите ___, потом поверните ___.
questions.explanation: прямо = straight, направо = to the right. Идите прямо, потом поверните направо.

rules: Asking location: Где [place]? or Скажите, где [place]?
Direction adverbs: прямо (straight), направо (right), налево (left) — invariable
Direction imperatives: идите (go on foot), поверните (turn)
summary.nextLesson: Next: shopping and transactions — buying things, asking prices, and practical number use.

questions.prompt: Excuse me where is the bank? (polite, formal): ___, пожалуйста, где банк?
The station is next to the metro: Вокзал рядом ___ метро.
